items.title: Build long strings with join
items.prefer: Append to a list and call `''.join(parts)` once at the end.
items.why: O(n) vs O(n²); the difference is dramatic for streaming text.
items.example:
```python
buf = []
for chunk in stream():
    buf.append(chunk)
text = ''.join(buf)
```

items.title: Encode/decode at the boundary
items.avoid: Holding bytes through the middle of business logic.
items.prefer: Decode at I/O entry, work in `str`, encode at I/O exit.
items.why: Keeps text semantics consistent and avoids `TypeError: cannot concatenate str and bytes`.
items.example:
```python
raw = response.content
text = raw.decode('utf-8')
response_bytes = output.encode('utf-8')
```

items.title: Use a tokenizer for token-aware decisions
items.avoid: Sizing prompts or estimating costs from `len(text)`.
items.prefer: Encode with the model's tokenizer and count its output.
items.why: Tokens and characters are different units; cost and context limits are per token.

items.title: Use the right method to remove an affix
items.why: `strip` treats its argument as a *set of chars*, not a substring; it silently does the wrong thing.

items.title: Use f-strings for safe-to-interpolate text only
items.avoid: f-string-built SQL / HTML / shell commands with untrusted input.
items.why: f-strings don't escape; injection risks come from the *destination*, not Python.
